scenario: Standard iteration: snapshot after structured requirements committed
expected_behavior: Extract iteration goal (1 sentence), list Must-Have REQs with AC counts, extract Out of Scope, define key constraints (tech stack, performance targets, security), generate 1-page CONTEXT_SNAPSHOT.md at project root, commit with message 'docs: add context snapshot for editor iteration', notify user snapshot ready for Stage 3
notes: Must be ≤50 lines. Only facts from committed docs, no speculation. Include Forbidden Actions section.

scenario: Snapshot generation when requirements NOT yet committed
expected_behavior: STOP. Do NOT generate snapshot from uncommitted draft. Return message: 'Requirements doc not yet committed. Please confirm you have reviewed and approved docs/specs/2026-05-19-notifications-requirements.md, then I will commit it before generating snapshot'
notes: Snapshot is derived from committed source of truth, not draft. This prevents snapshot drifting from actual requirements later.

scenario: New team member onboarding: reading snapshot to understand iteration

input.context: needs to understand iteration goal and constraints quickly

expected_behavior: Snapshot is agent-readable and must enable fast scan: iteration goal clear in 1 sentence, Must-Have REQs listed with AC counts (enables test estimation), Out of Scope prevents scope creep, Key Constraints (tech stack, perf targets, security) guide implementation, Forbidden Actions prevent out-of-bounds work
notes: Snapshot must be scannable in <2 minutes. If >50 lines, engineer skips it entirely. Brevity is essential for downstream Stage 3-7 agents.
